# pw-router

> Minimal, auditable LLM routing gateway for regulated environments.

pw-router is a stateless OpenAI-compatible API gateway that routes requests to multiple LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, vLLM/RunPod) with circuit breakers, fallback chains, and pluggable middleware. Built for financial services, healthcare, and any regulated industry where the entire codebase must be auditable.

## Key Facts

- Language: Python 3.12, FastAPI
- License: MIT
- Core: ~1,100 lines across 8 files
- Dependencies: 4 (fastapi, uvicorn, httpx, pyyaml) — pinned via requirements.lock
- Status: v0.1.0 — production-ready, security-hardened
- Repo: github.com/Protocol-Wealth/pw-router
- Live: pw-router.fly.dev (ORD region)

## API Surface

- GET / — service info, version, available endpoints
- POST /v1/chat/completions — OpenAI-compatible chat (streaming + non-streaming)
- GET /v1/models — list available models (filtered by API key)
- GET /health — router health + per-model circuit breaker status
- GET /robots.txt — disallow all crawling
- GET /.well-known/security.txt — RFC 9116 security contact

## Architecture

Requests flow: Auth → Body sanitization (field allowlist) → Pre-middleware → Router (model select + circuit breaker) → Provider Adapter → Post-middleware → Response.

Provider adapters translate between OpenAI format and provider-native formats. The Anthropic adapter handles full request/response translation including streaming SSE event conversion.

Circuit breakers are per-model, in-memory (CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN). Fallback chains automatically route to the next healthy model.

Middleware plugins are Python modules (restricted to plugins.* namespace) with pre_request/post_response async hooks that can modify requests, add routing tags, or block traffic. Plugin load failures are fatal (fail-closed).

## Security

- Request body sanitization: allowlist of permitted fields, unknown fields stripped
- Constant-time API key comparison (hmac.compare_digest)
- 10MB body size enforced on actual bytes (not Content-Length header)
- 5-minute max stream duration timeout
- Plugin loading restricted to plugins.* namespace only
- Security response headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Cache-Control: no-store)
- Generic error messages to clients (no provider details leaked)
- Non-root container user, pinned dependency lockfile
- No disk writes, no telemetry, no analytics

## Configuration

Single YAML file with ${ENV_VAR} expansion. No database. Models, routing rules, fallback chains, and middleware all configured declaratively.

## Documentation

- Full docs: llms-full.txt
- Architecture: docs/architecture.md
- Plugin guide: docs/plugins.md
- Deployment: docs/deployment.md
- Contributing: CONTRIBUTING.md
- Security: SECURITY.md
